_comment: Epic Q baselines (ADR 0030). STRUCTURAL values gate HARD (exact, or summaryChars ± tolerancePct): a red gate is a real product change — update the baseline consciously, with reasoning in the commit, never by auto-regeneration. softCeilingsMs are ~10×-class sanity ceilings for wall-clock (generous on purpose; csharp/mixed cover a cold sidecar build incl. NuGet restore). csharp-small and mixed encode the Roslyn tier and are only gated where the dotnet CLI exists.

coldStartSla._comment: ADR 0034 S4 — conversational COLD-START latency SLAs, gated by test/latencySla.test.ts. Each target is built in a FRESH node process (true cold start: WASM tree-sitter grammars + the TS Program load from scratch — the two cold-start drivers ADR 0034 names). Unlike the per-fixture softCeilingsMs (a ~10× wall-clock SANITY ceiling on a within-process init), these are real product budgets: the first deep tool call must return under slaMs even on a slow CI runner. Honoring ADR 0030's anti-flakiness mandate, slaMs keeps generous headroom over devBaselineMs (machine-relative; a slow runner needing a bump is a conscious edit here, with reasoning — never auto-regenerated). THREE complementary targets cover distinct cold-start failure modes: 'cpp-namespaces' = the WASM grammar-load path; 'self' = this repository (a realistic ~144-file multi-language medium repo: TS source + cpp/python/… fixtures → WASM), gitignore-scoped, the hardest real-world COMPLEXITY cold-start (coldMs grows with the repo, so a breach is a genuine regression signal); 'large' = a GENERATED 1000-file synthetic TS repo (temp dir, cleaned up) gating cold-start SCALING in file COUNT — it catches an O(n²)-class regression in the walk/graph-build/index. NOTE: file count is NOT the dominant cold-start cost (1000 trivial synthetic files ~0.5s « 144 real multi-language files ~2.8s) — 'large' is a count-scaling guard, not a complexity test; a faithful large-real-repo SLA would still need a representative repo.
